_doc: Shared conformance fixtures for the ZTL Connector core judge (SPEC v0.1 §4). A consumer's native re-implementation must produce the same expected verdict record on each warrant. Expected outputs were derived from ZTL semantics and confirmed against the Python reference. Covers grounded T/F, contradiction, on-credit, fail-closed (unknown value / inadmissible), default-deny (missing atom), and disjunction.

fixtures.name: grounded-true
fixtures.description: Both atoms decided; conjunction true; hereditary -> EARNED.

fixtures.name: grounded-false
fixtures.description: Both atoms decided; conjunction false; hereditary -> REFUTED.

fixtures.name: contradiction
fixtures.description: p & ~p is false under every reading; hereditary -> REFUTED.

fixtures.name: on-credit
fixtures.description: Double negation over an unverified atom is true only on credit; the mark rides -> ON CREDIT.

fixtures.name: fail-closed-unknown-value
fixtures.description: A value outside {T,F} ('approved') is coerced to Z; the claim is not established -> OPEN.

fixtures.name: fail-closed-inadmissible
fixtures.description: A decided value with admissible='no' is coerced to Z -> OPEN. The admissibility gate.

fixtures.name: default-deny-missing-atom
fixtures.description: An atom named in the claim but absent from the list defaults to Z (default-deny) -> OPEN, naming the weak link.

fixtures.name: disjunction-earned
fixtures.description: Both atoms decided; disjunction true; hereditary -> EARNED.
